Meta.title: Hack Club — Where teens make cool stuff.
Meta.description: Hack Club is the world's largest nonprofit movement of teenagers making cool projects.

Common.termsAgree: For all teens aged 13–18. By continuing, you agree to our

Home.subtext: Hack Club is the world's largest nonprofit movement of teenagers making cool projects.

Home.donorsTitle: Supported by people who believe in teens.
Home.donorsFree: Hack Club is free for every teenager, forever.
Home.donorsOpen: Hack Club is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Our finances are fully open — anyone can
Home.donorsSeeSpend: see exactly how money is spent.
Home.donorsBelieve: If you believe in what we're building,
Home.joiningSubtext: Hack Club isn't just events — here's what you get as part of the community.
Home.joiningSlackBody1: Chat, collaborate, and hang out with tens of thousands of teen makers from around the world. Find people working on the same weird projects as you, get help when you're stuck, or just #lounge.
Home.joiningSlackBody2: We also host AMAs with people like Sal Khan, George Hotz, and Lady Ada — people we really wanted to talk to, so we just invited them.
Home.joiningHcbBody1: Got an idea for a hackathon, club, or event? HCB gives your team the financial tools to accept donations, manage money, and operate under a real 501(c)(3) umbrella — no paperwork nightmare.
Home.joiningHcbBody2: Already used by 700+ teenager-led teams running world-class events
Home.joiningClubsBody1: There are Hack Clubs at high schools all over the world. Each one is a place where you build real projects from day one — no boring lectures, no prerequisites.
Home.joiningClubsBody2: Find one near you, or start your own and we'll help you get it off the ground.
Home.joiningPerksBody: GitHub Education, Brilliant Premium, and a bunch more — all unlocked just by being part of Hack Club. No catch, no cost.
Home.readyBody: Get started with building something real.

Press.metaDescription: Hack Club press resources, media coverage, and official logos for press use.
Press.heroLede: Hack Club is a global nonprofit network of high school makers & student-led coding clubs where young people build the agency, the network, & the technical talent to think big & do big things in the world.
Press.fact1: Global nonprofit network of teen makers.
Press.aboutCopy: Founded in 2014 by 16-year-old Zach Latta, Hack Clubs are now in nearly 1,400 high schools with 100,000 teenagers each year. Hack Club has been profiled on the TODAY Show, in the Wall Street Journal, and in many other publications around the country.
Press.inquiriesCopy: If you are writing a story or have other press inquiries, please contact Christina Asquith.
Press.photosCopy: Download and use these images for coverage of Hack Club programs and events.
Press.logosCopy: Use these official logo files for Hack Club HQ. Stick to SVG, PNG, or PDF. Avoid stretching, outlining, or recoloring.
Press.needMoreCopy: Reach out for photography, event branding, or co-branded work.

Philosophy.metaDescription: Hack Club's philosophy on coding, learning, and building a community of teenage makers.
Philosophy.beliefsTitle: A few beliefs that shape how we build.
Philosophy.joinBody: Start a Hack Club at your school or jump straight into our worldwide community of young makers.

0.title: Coding is a
0.body: Learning to code is uniquely like gaining a superpower: it converts you from a consumer to a creator. Suddenly, computers become a tool for creating.

1.title: Make, from anywhere.
1.body: There's never been a better time for making: anywhere in the world, anyone with a laptop and an internet connection can learn to make an app. Building things has never been so globally democratized.

2.title: Hack, hack, hack.
2.leadText: The goal of Hack Club is to help you become a hacker.
2.body: We want a space at every school where people are making interesting things with code, every week. Most schools don't provide that, so we're creating it in every school to make building things accessible to everyone.

3.title: Start building.
3.body: Most coding classes teach you programming concepts instead of how to write real code. It's like trying to learn carpentry without any wood. So at Hack Club, you learn to code entirely through building things. You start with no experience and build and ship a project every meeting.

4.title: Learn as you build.
4.bodyPrefix: Just as the best carpenters didn't learn in the classroom, neither did the best programmers. Through our
4.bodySuffix: you'll be walked through building projects. Starting out, you won't understand how the code works, but you'll build understanding as you go. You'll get stuck along the way, but we're here to help.

5.title: Be part of a community.
5.body: Hack Club gives you a worldwide community of thousands of other young makers to talk to. We're artists, writers, engineers, tinkerers, campers, filmmakers, volunteers. We make things. We help one another. We have fun. Join us.

Clubs.metaTitle: Hack Club Clubs — Start a Club
Clubs.metaDescription: Start a student-led coding club with Hack Club. Get workshops, mentorship, events, tools, and a global community.
Clubs.heroTitle1: Don't run your coding club alone.
Clubs.heroSubtext: A global nonprofit network of high school coding clubs where teens build technical skills, real projects, and momentum.
Clubs.rundownTitle: Clubs discovering the joy of code.
Clubs.rundownBody: Hack Clubs meet weekly in schools and communities. Leaders kick things off, members build websites, apps, and games at their own pace, then everyone demos what they made.
Clubs.step1Body: Leaders introduce a project direction and get everyone started.
Clubs.step2Body: Members work independently and help each other ship something real.
Clubs.step3Body: Showing your work creates momentum, confidence, and community.
Clubs.rundownFooter: Clubs also run and attend hackathons, join year-long programs, and grow with other student leaders worldwide.
Clubs.welcomeTitle: By the students, for the students.
Clubs.welcomeBody: Learning to code should feel creative and social, not like another class. Every Hack Club is student-led and project-driven.
Clubs.runningTitle: Get your club going and growing.
Clubs.featureLeaderBody: Join thousands of leaders in the Hack Club Slack to share ideas, ask questions, and meet peers in <channel>#leaders</channel>.
Clubs.featureWorkshopsBody: Use ready-to-run meeting content so beginners can build websites, games, bots, and more at <jams>Hack Club Jams</jams>.
Clubs.featureStickersBody: We ship physical materials to help you recruit members and create club culture fast.
Clubs.featureFundingBody: Raise and manage money through Hack Club's nonprofit infrastructure and spend on what your club needs.
Clubs.featureEventsBody: AMAs, hack nights, lock-ins, and weird internet events keep your members inspired all year.
Clubs.featureExistingBody: Already running a CS club? You can join Hack Club and keep your current name while using all our <resources>resources</resources>.
Clubs.startBody: It's online, free, and takes less than an hour to kick off.
Clubs.applyStep1Body: Tell us about your school and who is leading.
Clubs.applyStep2Body: Get approval from your school for the club.
Clubs.applyStep3Body: Schedule your first session and start shipping projects.

Jobs.metaDescription: Join the Hack Club team. View open positions and apply to work with us.
Jobs.heroLede: We are a nonprofit building the best programs and adventures for teenage makers around the world. If you love making things, you will feel right at home.
Jobs.emptyTitle: No open roles at this time. Check back later!
Jobs.emptyBody: Interested in working with us? Email us at team@hackclub.com with how you'd like to contribute.
